response:
THE ART GALLERY SHOWCASES DIGITAL INSTALLATIONS THAT MERGE MATH AND DESIGN.
THE FIRST PIECE USES A PARAMETRIC EQUATION TO GENERATE GEOMETRIC PATTERNS ON SCREENS.
FOR THE PATTERN TO FORM A CLOSED LOOP, THE RATIO OF A TO B MUST BE RATIONAL.
LET A OVER B EQUAL P OVER Q WHERE P AND Q ARE CO-PRIME INTEGERS.
THE PHASE SHIFT PHI CAN BE ANY REAL NUMBER WITHOUT AFFECTING THE CLOSED NATURE.
THE PERIOD T OF THIS MOTION IS TWO PI TIMES Q DIVIDED BY THE GREATEST COMMON DIVISOR.
THE SECOND INSTALLATION DISPLAYS A THREE DIMENSIONAL SURFACE DEFINED BY AN IMPLICIT FUNCTION.
CHANGES IN THE PARAMETER LAMBDA ALTER THE CURVATURE AND TOPOLOGY OF THIS VISUAL FORM.
THE CRITICAL VALUES OCCUR WHEN THE EIGENVALUES OF THE ASSOCIATED MATRIX CHANGE SIGN.
GOOD RESULTS ARE SEEN WHEN THE SURFACE TRANSITIONS FROM A SPHERE TO A COMPLEX SHAPE.
THE SURFACE REMAINS CONNECTED FOR SMALL VALUES BUT SPLIT INTO TWO PARTS AT EXTREMES.
VISUAL APPEARANCE SHIFTS FROM A SIMPLE BLOB TO A STARLIKE FORM WITH PROTRUDING TIPS.
JOURNALISTS MUST NOTE HOW THESE MATHEMATICAL RULES CREATE BEAUTIFUL AND DYNAMIC ART.
NO FINAL WORDS ARE NEEDED TO CONCLUDE THIS REVIEW OF THE TECHNOLOGY DRIVEN EXHIBITION.
THE EXHIBITION STANDS AS A TESTAMENT TO THE POWER OF ALGORITHMS IN MODERN CREATIVE WORKS.
